title: pyrus carolinensis var. ellipsoidalis
intro: Pyrus carolinensis var. ellipsoidalis, also known as the Appalachian pear, is a variety of pear tree native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its small, ellipsoidal fruit. The bark, leaves, and fruit of the Appalachian pear have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pyrus carolinensis var. ellipsoidalis include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
